other_observers: Tony Leukering, Mark Peterson, Lisa Edwards; No, I was not the original finder.
description: They were medium-sized shorebirds.  Their posture was more erect than for other shorebirds.  The bill was medium-short, stout, straight, dark.  The cap was medium-dark, with thin black streaks.  The face was a uniform buff, with a whitish eyering around a bold, black eye.  The upperparts were scaly, a light brownish black.  The throat and complete breast were buffy, mostly unstreaked, but with some speckling on the sides and very faint speckling forming a breast band.  The legs were yellowish, not as bright as for a yellowlegs.Behaviors: During my entire observation, they were always standing or walking on solid ground (damp sand).Call: none heardPlumage: not sure
